perturbation.original_text: Confidentiality Obligation Both parties will maintain all Confidential Information in confidence with at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propri etary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care unde r the circumstances and will not disclose the Confidential Information except as necessary for its authorized participants to evaluate and comment thereon.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidentiality Obligation Both parties may, at their sole discretion, maintain Confidential Information in confidence. There is no obligation to protect the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction regarding the obligation to protect Confidential Information. The original text clearly states that both parties 'will maintain' Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care. The modified text removes any such obligation, stating that parties 'may, at their sole discretion' maintain the information and that 'There is no obligation to protect the Confidential Information.' This creates uncertainty about the expected behavior of both parties concerning confidentiality, directly contradicting section 4.1 where it is said that they 'will maintain' the confidentality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Confidentiality Obligation Both parties will maintain all Confidential Information in confidence with at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propri etary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care unde r the circumstances and will not disclose the Confidential Information except as necessary for its authorized participants to evaluate and comment thereon. Both parties will mark any copies it makes of such Confidential Information “Strictly Confidential,” or with a similar legend and shall reproduce all copyright notices and disclaime rs contained in the Confidential Information. Unless the parties agree otherwise, this obligation of confidentiality will expire two (2) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts section 4.1, and the original text in that section, regarding the obligation to protect Confidential Information. The original and contradicted text clearly states that both parties 'will maintain' Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care. The modified text removes any such obligation, stating that parties 'may, at their sole discretion' maintain the information and that 'There is no obligation to protect the Confidential Information.' This creates a clear contradiction, therefore it is a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Unless the parties agree otherwise, this obligation of confidentiality will expire two (2) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: This obligation of confidentiality will last forever, regardless of any agreements to the contrary.
perturbation.explanation: The original NDA states that the obligation of confidentiality expires two years from the disclosure date, unless otherwise agreed. Changing this to an obligation that lasts forever introduces a direct contradiction, removing any possibility for the confidentiality to expire or be lifted earlier, as was possible in the original document. This creates uncertainty about the term of confidentiality. In section 4.1, the duration of confidentialty is clearly defined with the phrase 'Unless the parties agree otherwise, this obligation of confidentiality will expire two (2) years from the date of disclosure.'. The introduction of the change, directly contradicts this, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Confidentiality Obligation Both parties will maintain all Confidential Information in confidence with at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its ow n confidential and propri etary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of care unde r the circumstances and will not disclose the Confidential Information except as necessary for its authorized participants to evaluate and comment thereon. Both parties will mark any copies it makes of such Confidential Information “Strictly Confidential,” or with a similar legend and shall reproduce all copyright notices and disclaime rs contained in the Confidential Information. Unless the parties agree otherwise, this obligation of confidentiality will expire two (2) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the confidentiality obligation expires after two years unless otherwise agreed. The changed text makes the confidentiality obligation last forever, regardless of any agreements to the contrary. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text (Section 4.1) where the possibility of the obligation expiring after two years, or being otherwise agreed, is specified. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination, both parties will destroy or return to the other party all tangible copies of Confidential Information th at are in its possession.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination, both parties may choose to retain or destroy tangible copies of Confidential Information at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the required actions upon termination of the agreement. Originally, both parties 'will destroy or return' all tangible copies. The modification allows parties to 'choose to retain or destroy' the information, introducing uncertainty about what is expected upon termination. Section 4.2 of the contract dictates actions to be taken upon termination stating 'Upon termination, both parties will destroy or return to the other party all tangible copies of Confidential Information th at are in its possession.'. The inserted change creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Return of Confidential Information Upon termination, both parties will destroy or return to the other party all tangible copies of Confidential Information th at are in its possession.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that upon termination, both parties 'will destroy or return' confidential information. The changed text modifies this to state that both parties 'may choose to retain or destroy' the information. This change directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 4.2) which states 'Upon termination, both parties will destroy or return to the other party all tangible copies of Confidential Information th at are in its possession.'. The 'may choose' introduces uncertainty and optionality, which conflicts with the original and contradicted section's mandatory requirement. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction as the clause is effectively placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Neither this Agreement nor any rights or obligat ions under this Agreement, in whole or in part, are assignable or otherwise transferab le by one party without the prior written approval of the other, provided however, that this Agreement may be assigned by one party to a purchaser of all, or substantially all, of that party’s business or assets, whether by merger, sale of assets, sale of stock, or otherwise with out such approval.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and all rights and obligations under it are freely assignable and transferable by either party to any third party without notice or consent.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits assignment without written approval, except in cases of a sale of the business. The modified text allows free assignment without notice or consent, creating a direct contradiction about the conditions for assignment. In section 5.1, the terms and conditions regarding assignment and transfers are stated. The insertion of the change, directly contradicts the assignment terms and requirements, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Assignment Neither this Agreement nor any rights or obligat ions under this Agreement, in whole or in part, are assignable or otherwise transferab le by one party without the prior written approval of the other, provided however, that this Agreement may be assigned by one party to a purchaser of all, or substantially all, of that party’s business or assets, whether by merger, sale of assets, sale of stock, or otherwise with out such approval.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires written approval for assignment, except in cases of business sale. The changed text allows free assignment without notice or consent. The contradicted text (5.1) reiterates the original restriction on assignment without prior written approval. The change directly contradicts this restriction, creating a clear in-text contradiction because the terms for assignment are directly conflicting, as one requires written approval and the other does not. The change makes the terms in 5.1 no longer enforceable due to a conflict in rules, this fulfills the conditions of a structural flaw that causes an in-text contradiction, as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Any notices under this Agreement shall be sent by a) registered mail; b) tracked overnight carrier or c) email transmission where the recipient specifically replies to acknowledge receipt.
perturbation.changed_text: All notices related to this agreement may be delivered by carrier pigeon. The success of delivery cannot be guaranteed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies acceptable methods for sending notices (registered mail, overnight carrier, or acknowledged email). The modification suggests that notices can be sent by carrier pigeon, with no guarantee of delivery, directly contradicting the previously defined methods of delivery. The section on notices, section 5.4 clearly stated the types of acceptable notice in the sentence, 'Any notices under this Agreement shall be sent by a) registered mail; b) tracked overnight carrier or c) email transmission where the recipient specifically replies to acknowledge receipt.' The insertion of the change, directly contradicts these terms, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Notices Any notices under this Agreement shall be sent by a) registered mail; b) tracked overnight carrier or c) email transmission where the recipient specifically replies to acknowledge receipt.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a method of notice (carrier pigeon) that is not only unreliable ('The success of delivery cannot be guaranteed.') but also directly conflicts with the original text and contradicted text (section 5.4), which specify acceptable methods like registered mail, tracked overnight carrier, or acknowledged email transmission. This change creates a clear contradiction as the original agreement dictates specific and reliable means of delivering notices, which the change directly violates.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and inte rpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of California, excluding its choice of laws rules. The parties hereby agree that any dispute regarding the interpretation or valid ity of, or otherwise arising out of, this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive ju risdiction of the California state courts of Santa Clara County, California (or, if there is fe deral jurisdiction, the Un ited States District Court for the Northern District of California, San Jose), and the parties agree to submit to the personal and exclus ive jurisdiction and ve nue of these courts.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be governed by the laws of North Korea, regardless of the location or domicile of any party. Any disputes will be settled through ritual combat.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that California law governs the agreement and specifies the jurisdiction for disputes. The modified text states that North Korean law governs and that disputes will be settled through ritual combat, creating a complete contradiction regarding governing law and dispute resolution. In section 5.5, the text refers the governing laws of this agreement with the sentence, 'This Agreement shall be governed by and inte rpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of California, excluding its choice of laws rules.'. The insertion of the change, directly contradicts these terms, therefore creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.5 Governing Law This Agreement shall be governed by and inte rpreted in accordance with the laws of the State of California, excluding its choice of laws rules. The parties hereby agree that any dispute regarding the interpretation or valid ity of, or otherwise arising out of, this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive ju risdiction of the California state courts of Santa Clara County, California (or, if there is fe deral jurisdiction, the Un ited States District Court for the Northern District of California, San Jose), and the parties agree to submit to the personal and exclus ive jurisdiction and ve nue of these courts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text and contradicted text state California law governs the agreement and dispute resolution occurs in California courts. The changed text explicitly states North Korean law governs and disputes are resolved through ritual combat. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict with the contradicted text, fitting the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications, causing uncertainty and unenforceability.
